headline: Colin Hanks Resurrects John Candy's Legacy in Intimate Doc Premiere
lede: Actor-turned-documentarian Colin Hanks delivers a revelatory portrait of comedy legend John Candy, promising to reframe audience perceptions of the beloved performer.
body:
Colin Hanks isn't just making another celebrity documentary — he's excavating the nuanced humanity behind one of comedy's most cherished icons.

The festival circuit darling prepares to unveil "John Candy: I Like Me," a meticulously crafted exploration of the late comedian's life that transcends standard biographical storytelling. Hanks approaches the project with surgical precision and deep reverence, promising viewers an unprecedented look into Candy's creative process and personal struggles.

Drawing from extensive archival footage and interviews with Candy's closest collaborators, the documentary reconstructs the trajectory of a performer who defined an entire comedic era. Rare behind-the-scenes materials and personal correspondence illuminate the complex inner world of an actor often typecast as merely a lovable big man.

Hanks' commitment to authenticity distinguishes this project from standard hagiographic tributes. By refusing to sanitize Candy's narrative, the filmmaker reveals the profound vulnerabilities and artistic ambitions that drove the comedian's remarkable career.

The documentary arrives at a crucial moment of cultural reconsideration, positioning Candy not just as a comedic figure, but as a transformative artistic voice who challenged Hollywood's narrow representations of body and humor.
key_facts: First feature documentary about John Candy by Colin Hanks
Explores Candy's personal and professional complexities
